title: source_class/v0 record
description: A record on the source-class-v0 wire. This schema validates STRUCTURE, not verdict: a record with an unrecognized source_class is well-formed (a reader folds it to `unknown`), and a record with no source_class is well-formed (a reader reports `undeclared`). The one structural invariant a schema can carry is that a digest travels with the canonicalization id that produced it; a digest without its scheme id is rejected here, matching the `invalid` verdict a conforming reader returns for it.

outcome.description: What the record claims happened. Free string; the axis types the BASIS of the claim, not its value.

source_class.description: The basis the outcome claim rests on. Recognized values: producer_reported, issuer_attested, receiver_receipt, boundary_observed, third_party_observed, independently_observed, unknown. NOT enum-constrained on purpose: an unrecognized value is structurally valid and a reader folds it to `unknown` fail-closed, so a closed enum here would reject records the vocabulary treats as well-formed.

observer.description: Optional prose describing the vantage that produced the observation (e.g. a kernel tracepoint the observed process cannot write to).

coverage_boundary.description: Optional declared boundary the claim is scoped to. An observed class covers the named boundary only.

outcome_digest.description: Optional content address of the outcome record. If present, the canonicalization id MUST travel with the digest (invariant 3): two readers with different implicit canonicalizations derive different bytes for the same record and cannot tell whose reading was meant.

digest.description: Hash of the referenced record, e.g. sha256:<hex>.

canonicalization.description: The scheme the digest was computed under, e.g. jcs/rfc8785 or cbor/rfc8949. Neither is the default; it is declared per record.
